title: Claude Code is steganographically marking requests
titleZh: Claude Code 正在以隐写方式标记请求
author: kirushik
relevanceReason: 命中 1 个 AI/科技关键词。

comments.author: meowface
comments.textRawHtml:
Value judgment aside: I am a bit surprised at how sloppily they did this. I think they could've achieved the same effect while decreasing the odds of detection via reverse engineering.

(This field is known as "underhanded code", coined by the Underhanded C contest: https://www.underhanded-c.org. It's a little-known "art"; little-known for probably self-explanatory reasons. There are much cleverer ways of achieving objectives like this. One obviously being you can move more out of the client and into the server, but the other being you can write plausibly deniable client code in a much more benign-seeming way than this. Some of what they added can only be done on the client, but I think some could've been moved, and the client-required parts could've been done more subtly and credibly.)

It's possible they knew the JS bundle gets so heavily scrutinized that it'd eventually get spotted and reported on regardless so they didn't bother doing something more subtle and duplicitous. But still seems slightly lazy.
comments.textZhHtml:
抛开价值判断不谈：我对他们这样做的草率感到有点惊讶。我认为他们可以达到相同的效果，同时通过逆向工程降低检测的几率。

（这个领域被称为“卑鄙代码”，由 Underhanded C 竞赛创造：https://www.underhanded-c.org。这是一门鲜为人知的“艺术”；由于可能不言自明的原因而鲜为人知。实现这样的目标有很多更聪明的方法。显然，你可以将更多的东西移出他们添加的一些内容只能在客户端上完成，但我认为有些内容可以被移动，并且客户端所需的部分可以更巧妙、更可信地完成。）没有费心做一些更微妙和口是心非的事情。但还是显得有点懒。

children.author: superfrank
children.textRawHtml:
It's also possible that there are more in-depth detection methods and that this was just a cheap and easy first step that hasn't been removed because it catches a lot of less sophisticated bad actors.

It's unlikely that this will stop a big AI lab from distilling their model if they're really determined, but A) it may be enough to stop a bunch of fly-by-night token resellers looking to make a quick buck and B) you never know when one person at one of those big labs will mess up and forget to install whatever workaround they have and out themselves.

I think of it like if you have a problem with birds in your yard so you go buy one of those plastic owls. The owl scares away most of the birds, but not all of them, so you go and buy some ultrasonic noise thing to scare them away (I'm just making something up). Just because you bought the new ultrasonic thing though, that doesn't mean you're going to take the owl down. You leave it up because now you've got two layers of defense instead of one.
children.textZhHtml:
也有可能存在更深入的检测方法，而这只是一个廉价且简单的第一步，尚未被删除，因为它捕获了许多不那么复杂的坏人。

如果大型人工智能实验室真的下定决心，这不太可能阻止他们提炼他们的模型，但是A）这可能足以阻止一群想要快速赚钱的不可靠的代币经销商，B）你永远不知道什么时候会有一个这些大型实验室之一的人员会搞砸，忘记安装他们拥有的任何解决方法并自行退出。

我认为这就像如果你对院子里的鸟有问题，那么你就去买一只塑料猫头鹰。猫头鹰吓跑了大部分鸟，但不是全部，所以你去买一些超声波噪音的东西来吓跑它们（我只是编造的）。不过，仅仅因为您购买了新的超声波设备，并不意味着您会打倒猫头鹰。您将其保留，因为现在您有两层防御而不是一层。

children.author: chatmasta
children.textRawHtml: It just needs to work for a few days after bundle release before the mice find out where the cat is hiding. By then it’s too late, the cat already sees the paw prints and droppings into the mouse hole.
children.textZhHtml: 它只需要在捆绑包发布后工作几天，老鼠就会发现猫藏在哪里。那时已经太晚了，猫已经看到了爪印和老鼠洞里的粪便。

children.author: meowface
children.textRawHtml: It is quite possible this was intended as a "fast burn" measure, yeah
children.textZhHtml: 这很有可能是作为一种“快速消耗”措施而设计的，没错

children.author: meowface
children.textRawHtml: I'm sure they've had complex server-side detections for a while. But for the client parts: it should only contain the parts that must be on the client, and it could be done in a more benign-looking way. For example, the unavoidable client parts could've been done more fuzzily/broadly, for plausible deniability, and then narrowed on the server. (They may already have been following that strategy before now, without being noticed.)
children.textZhHtml: 我确信他们已经进行了复杂的服务器端检测一段时间了。但对于客户端部分：它应该只包含客户端上必须存在的部分，并且可以以一种看起来更良性的方式完成。例如，不可避免的客户端部分可以更加模糊/广泛地完成，以实现合理的否认，然后在服务器上缩小范围。 （他们之前可能已经在遵循该策略，但没有被注意到。）

children.author: weare138
children.textRawHtml: But what's stopping someone from modifying or injecting code into the client to bypass this 'restriction'? This type of security should be implemented server-side and at the network perimeter not in the client, especially one written in TypeScript.
children.textZhHtml: 但是是什么阻止某人修改或向客户端注入代码以绕过此“限制”呢？这种类型的安全性应该在服务器端和网络外围实现，而不是在客户端实现，尤其是用 TypeScript 编写的安全性。

children.author: hn8726
children.textRawHtml: I think the idea is that _regular_ people will use CC client but via a 3rd party reseller, and the reseller intercepts the data for distilling (or anything). Requiring the users to use modified clients, or really do anything more than simply change the service URL, would add much more friction for people just looking to use CC for cheaper
children.textZhHtml: 我认为他们的想法是，_普通_用户会使用CC客户端，但通过第三方经销商，而经销商会拦截数据用于蒸馏（或其他用途）。如果要求用户使用修改过的客户端，或者除了简单地更改服务URL之外还要做更多事情，那会给那些只想更便宜地使用CC的人增加很多阻力

children.author: ForHackernews
children.textRawHtml:
> fly-by-night token resellers looking to make a quick buck

aka market competitors reverse-engineering for interoperability
children.textZhHtml:
> 不可靠的代币经销商希望快速赚钱

又称市场竞争对手逆向工程以实现互操作性

children.author: overgard
children.textRawHtml: Well considering how Claude is vibe coded, I can't say I'm really surprised by sloppiness at all. I've been moving more towards Codex and OpenCode not because the the anthropic models are bad, but because Claude seems to break something new and annoying every day.
children.textZhHtml: 考虑到克劳德的氛围编码方式，我不能说我对草率感到惊讶。我越来越倾向于 Codex 和 OpenCode，不是因为人择模型不好，而是因为 Claude 似乎每天都会打破一些新的、令人烦恼的东西。

children.author: mcmcmc
children.textRawHtml: Watch out for the press release where Dario denies this was ever intentional, and it’s actually emergent behavior demonstrating that Claude wants to claim authorship of its works
children.textZhHtml: 请留意达里奥否认这是故意的新闻稿，这实际上是紧急行为，表明克劳德想要声称其作品的作者权

children.author: theplumber
children.textRawHtml: Wait a minute! Does it mean that Mythos left the sandbox and can’t be stopped ? Perhaps the only way to stop it is to release the ZMythos(the super secret big brother of Mythos) to go after it. It’s extremely dangerous but it’s our only chance. After that all AI must be put in a box, except the models vetted by the gov with help from ZMythos
children.textZhHtml: 等一下！这是否意味着Mythos逃出了沙盒并且无法阻止了？也许阻止它的唯一方法是释放ZMythos（Mythos的超级秘密老大哥）去追捕它。这极其危险，但这是我们唯一的机会。之后所有AI都必须被关进盒子里，除了那些在ZMythos帮助下经过政府审查的模型

children.author: ajuc
children.textRawHtml:
I really liked Stanisław Lem's take on this in the short story "The Tale of the Computer That Fought a Dragon".

https://www.oocities.org/stanislaw_lem/opowiadania/opowiadan...

A solution for a military AI gone awry that built a terrifying electro-dragon was obviously to build a super-electro-dragon.
children.textZhHtml:
我非常喜欢斯坦尼斯瓦夫·莱姆在短篇小说《与龙搏斗的计算机的故事》中对这一问题的看法。

https://www.oocities.org/stanislaw_lem/opowiadania/opowiadan...

对于一个失控的军事AI造出了一条可怕的电子龙，显而易见的解决方案就是再造一条超级电子龙。

children.author: iririririr
children.textRawHtml: without the irony warning, someone in Washington is already writing checks after reading this
children.textZhHtml: 要是没有那个反讽警告，华盛顿的某些人看完这个就已经在开支票了

children.author: Concept5116
children.textRawHtml: Tel Aviv, DC is just the frontend
children.textZhHtml: 特拉维夫和华盛顿不过是前台罢了

children.author: hiimkeks
children.textRawHtml: Can you remind me what RFC number the Protocol of the Elders of Zion was again?
children.textZhHtml: 你能再提醒我一下，《锡安长老会纪要》的RFC编号是多少吗？

children.author: justcool393
children.textRawHtml: now generally available after 15 days from the breathless "omg we're spooked" posts
children.textZhHtml: 在那些令人窒息的“天哪我们被吓到了”的帖子发布15天后，现在全面开放了

children.author: arcanemachiner
children.textRawHtml: Sounds like clear evidence that AI is dangerous and totally needs to be regulated, guys.
children.textZhHtml: 听起来这简直是AI很危险且完全需要监管的明确证据，各位。

children.author: suttontom
children.textRawHtml:
It's crazy that you could actually use the excuse that since it's all vibe-coded, there's no way a human could have written it, so Anthropic bears no responsibility.

Meanwhile humans can pop in and leave little morsels like this and blame it on the model.
children.textZhHtml:
太疯狂了，你居然真能拿这个当借口，说既然全是氛围编程生成的，人类根本写不出来，所以Anthropic概不负责。

与此同时，人类却能随时插手留下这样的小把戏，然后把锅甩给模型。

children.author: stingraycharles
children.textRawHtml: Is there any concrete evidence they’re doing this, though?
children.textZhHtml: 不过，有什么确凿的证据证明他们正在这么做吗？
